options.label: A
options.text: reflect infrared radiation but absorb ultraviolet radiation.

options.label: B
options.text: reflect ultraviolet radiation but absorb infrared radiation.

options.label: C
options.text: transmit infrared radiation but absorb ultraviolet radiation.

options.label: D
options.text: transmit ultraviolet radiation but absorb infrared radiation.

vibe_explanation: The greenhouse effect relies on the fact that greenhouse gases are transparent to incoming short-wave radiation (like visible and UV) from the Sun, allowing it to reach the Earth's surface. However, they are highly effective at absorbing long-wave (infrared) radiation emitted by the Earth's surface, trapping heat in the atmosphere.
